# UC-22.3.24 · DORA ICT Concentration — Substitutability and Secondary Sourcing Readiness Index

## Description

Concentration risk mitigates when substitutable alternatives exist. Scoring each critical provider on contract portability, data export tested, and runbook completeness gives a defensible substitutability index for the register of information.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Calibrate weights with risk team; (2) refresh after DR tests; (3) link `provider_id` to RoI; (4) annual reassessment workflow; (5) do not embed legal advice—store lawyer sign-off reference externally. Lookups: `dora_substitutability`, `dora_substitutability.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: KV Store or `inputlookup dora_substitutability.csv`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `dora_substitutability.csv` (provider_id, contract_portable, last_export_test_date, runbook_score, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Calibrate weights with risk team; (2) refresh after DR tests; (3) link `provider_id` to RoI; (4) annual reassessment workflow; (5) do not embed legal advice—store lawyer sign-off reference externally.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup dora_substitutability.csv
| eval last_test=strptime(last_export_test_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval test_age_days=round((now()-last_test)/86400,0)
| eval readiness=contract_portable*0.4 + if(test_age_days<180,0.3,0) + runbook_score*0.3
| where readiness<0.6
| table provider_id, readiness, contract_portable, test_age_days, runbook_score
| sort readiness
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA ICT Concentration — Substitutability and Secondary Sourcing Readiness Index** — Concentration risk mitigates when substitutable alternatives exist. Scoring each critical provider on contract portability, data export tested, and runbook completeness gives a defensible substitutability index for the register of information.

Documented **Data sources**: `dora_substitutability.csv` (provider_id, contract_portable, last_export_test_date, runbook_score, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): KV Store or `inputlookup dora_substitutability.csv`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **last_test** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **test_age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **readiness** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where readiness<0.6` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: contract_portable, last_test, provider_id, readiness, runbook_score, test_age_days.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (readiness), Table (low scores), Single value (providers below bar).

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup dora_substitutability.csv
| eval last_test=strptime(last_export_test_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval test_age_days=round((now()-last_test)/86400,0)
| eval readiness=contract_portable*0.4 + if(test_age_days<180,0.3,0) + runbook_score*0.3
| where readiness<0.6
| table provider_id, readiness, contract_portable, test_age_days, runbook_score
| sort readiness
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (readiness), Table (low scores), Single value (providers below bar).
